title: draba incana subsp. loiseleurii
intro: Draba incana subsp. loiseleurii is a subspecies of the Draba incana plant, also known as the Alpine Whitlow Grass, a small, flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sulfur-containing compounds. The medicinal properties of Draba incana subsp. loiseleurii have been reported to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
